questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the UNIX standard?
options: A) To create a single, unified operating system
B) To provide a common interface for multiple operating systems
C) To establish a set of standards for UNIX implementation
D) To develop a new programming language
questions.explanation: The UNIX standard aims to establish a set of standards for UNIX implementation, ensuring compatibility and interoperability among different systems.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key aspect of the UNIX Standard?
questions.explanation: The UNIX Standard encompasses various aspects, including process synchronization, memory management, and file system organization.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of adopting the UNIX Standard?
questions.explanation: Adopting the UNIX Standard offers improved portability and interoperability, allowing systems to communicate and exchange data seamlessly.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a UNIX Standard?
options: C) The UNIX interface to the C programming language
questions.explanation: The UNIX Standard includes various components, such as the UNIX shell and the UNIX file system, which are designed to work together seamlessly.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the UNIX interface to the C programming language?
options: A) To provide a unified interface for C programming
B) To establish a set of standards for C programming
C) To enable C programs to run on UNIX systems
D) To provide a common interface for multiple programming languages
questions.explanation: The UNIX interface to the C programming language enables C programs to take advantage of UNIX features, such as process synchronization and file system organization.
